title: catalpa bignonioides var. nana
intro: Catalpa bignonioides var. nana, also known as the dwarf catalpa, is a compact or dwarf version of the catalpa tree. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America, known for its heart-shaped leaves and showy, bell-shaped flowers. The dwarf catalpa has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to lower fever and relieve coughs. Herbal preparations of catalpa bignonioides var. nana include tea, tincture, and infusions, which can be used to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
